perturbation.changed_text: 2. Purpose. Vapotherm agrees to disclose the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the section 'only for the following specific purpose (‘Permitted Purpose”): ________________________________________________________________________________________________ ________________________________________________________________________________________________', from the text, section 3.a, contradicts itself. The beginning of section 3, states 'The Receiving Party agrees that, in consideration of access to the Confidential Information, it shall hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall take all measures necessary to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of the persons not bound to this Agreement'. Then Section 3.a, states 'To use Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose;' which is contradicting itself as section 2 no longer provides what the permitted purpose is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Nondisclosure. The Receiving Party agrees that, in consideration of access to the Confidential Information, it shall hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall take all measures necessary to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of the persons not bound to this Agreement. The Receiving Party further agrees: a. To use Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose; b. Not to disclose Confidential Information to any other entity, except as expressly authorized in writing by Vapotherm; c. Not to use Confidential Information in such a way as to hinder, interfere with, or in any way circumvent Vapotherm’s interest in the Technology, or consulting or business relations between and among Vapotherm and third parties; d. Not to copy Confidential Information in whole or in part, without the express written permission of Vapotherm; e. To return Confidential Information, including all copies and records thereof, to Vapotherm, at the Receiving Party’s expense, within five (5) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within thirty (30) days following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2 defined a 'Permitted Purpose' for disclosing Confidential Information. The changed text removes this definition entirely. Section 3.a then contradicts itself because it refers to 'the Permitted Purpose' when Section 2 no longer defines what that purpose is. This creates a direct conflict and makes the clause unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Nondisclosure. The Receiving Party agrees that, in consideration of access to the Confidential Information, it shall hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall take all measures necessary to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of the persons not bound to this Agreement. The Receiving Party further agrees: a. To use Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose; b. Not to disclose Confidential Information to any other entity, except as expressly authorized in writing by Vapotherm; c. Not to use Confidential Information in such a way as to hinder, interfere with, or in any way circumvent Vapotherm’s interest in the Technology, or consulting or business relations between and among Vapotherm and third parties; d. Not to copy Confidential Information in whole or in part, without the express written permission of Vapotherm; e. To return Confidential Information, including all copies and records thereof, to Vapotherm, at the Receiving Party’s expense, within five (5) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within thirty (30) days following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Nondisclosure. The Receiving Party agrees that, in consideration of access to the Confidential Information, it shall hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence. The Receiving Party further agrees: a. To use Confidential Information; b. Not to disclose Confidential Information; c. Not to use Confidential Information in such a way as to hinder, interfere with, or in any way circumvent Vapotherm’s interest in the Technology, or consulting or business relations between and among Vapotherm and third parties; d. Not to copy Confidential Information; e. To return Confidential Information to Vapotherm.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'all measures necessary to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of the persons not bound to this Agreement.', 'for the Permitted Purpose', 'except as expressly authorized in writing by Vapotherm', 'in whole or in part', 'at the Receiving Party’s expense, within five (5) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within thirty (30) days following the termination of this Agreement', the language of this section is vague. The removal of specifics now contradicts section 5 'Injunctive Relief'. Section 5 relies on the 'terms of the Agreement' being clear and enforceable. However, with the omitted specifics, it is difficult to prove what constitutes a violation of the confidentiality obligations because the definitions of not to disclose or not to copy are no longer defined and vague. This weakens Vapotherm's ability to obtain injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Injunctive Relief. Because of the unique nature of the Confidential information, the Receiving Party understands and agrees that Vapotherm will suffer irreparable harm in the event that any party hereto fails to comply with any of the terms of the Agreement, and monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate such breach. Accordingly, the Receiving Party agrees that Vapotherm will, in addition to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, be entitled to injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 3, by omitting key phrases and stipulations found in the original text, creates vagueness and a lack of specificity. This directly contradicts Section 5, 'Injunctive Relief,' which relies on the clear and enforceable 'terms of the Agreement.' The omitted specifics make it difficult to prove a violation of confidentiality, thereby weakening Vapotherm's ability to obtain injunctive relief. The change directly impacts the enforceability mentioned in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with the constraints and obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Receiving Party’s Procedures. Receiving Party shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its employees, agents and independent contractors who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose. Receiving Party shall require all employees, agents and independent contractors who have access to Confidential Information to execute a confidentiality agreement limiting their use of such information to the Permitted Purpose and prohibiting them from disclosing such information to third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Receiving Party’s Procedures. Receiving Party shall disclose Confidential Information to its employees, agents and independent contractors. Receiving Party shall require all employees, agents and independent contractors who have access to Confidential Information to execute a confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'only to those of its employees, agents and independent contractors who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose' and 'limiting their use of such information to the Permitted Purpose and prohibiting them from disclosing such information to third parties', the changed text contradicts Section 6 'Misuse of Confidential Information'. If the receiving party is no longer obligated to restrict disclosure only to those with a 'need to know' and confidentiality agreements are not required to prevent disclosure to third parties, it becomes difficult to define and prove 'misuse'. Section 6 becomes toothless, because there is no way to enforce it. There are no restrictions anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Misuse of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall promptly advise Vapotherm, in writing, of any misappropriation or misuse of the Confidential Information, by any entity, which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the restrictions on who Confidential Information can be disclosed to and removes the requirement for confidentiality agreements to limit the use and disclosure of such information. This directly contradicts Section 6, which refers to 'Misuse of Confidential Information'. Without the restrictions in Section 4 of the original document, it would be impossible to enforce Section 6, as there are no longer parameters defined for what constitutes 'misuse'. The Changed Text creates a strong in-text contradiction, as described in the explanation and follows the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Inventions. Inventions means all discoveries, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others: (i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein. All Inventions will be immediately reported to Vapotherm and shall be the exclusive property of Vapotherm. The Receiving Party will perform, at Vapotherm’s request and expense, any act which it can reasonably perform that Vapotherm deems necessary to vest title to such inventions and ideas in Vapotherm and to execute any and all patent applications in connection therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Inventions. Inventions means all discoveries, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others. All Inventions will be immediately reported to Vapotherm and shall be the exclusive property of Vapotherm. The Receiving Party will perform, at Vapotherm’s request and expense, any act which it can reasonably perform that Vapotherm deems necessary to vest title to such inventions and ideas in Vapotherm and to execute any and all patent applications in connection therewith.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein' creates a contradiction in Section 16. 'Right in Confidential Information.' Section 16 states 'No license or other right is created or granted hereby, except the specific right to receive the Confidential Information and evaluate it as set forth herein'. However, by broadening the scope of 'Inventions' to include anything conceived, developed, or reduced to practice, regardless of its direct relationship to the Confidential Information or Permitted Purpose, the Receiving Party's existing rights to their own pre-existing or independently developed inventions are potentially infringed upon. The Receiving Party's pre-existing rights are now potentially undermined by the overbroad definition of inventions as now stated in Section 7. It doesn't take in consideration if the invention was made independenlty from the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. Right in Confidential Information. No license or other right is created or granted hereby, except the specific right to receive the Confidential Information and evaluate it as set forth herein, nor shall any license or any other right with respect to the subject matter hereof be created or granted except by written agreement signed by the duly authorized representative of each of the parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7 broadens the definition of 'Inventions' by removing the specific conditions under which inventions are considered Vapotherm's property. This contradicts Section 16, which states that no right is created except the right to receive and evaluate Confidential Information. The broader definition in Section 7 now potentially encompasses inventions created independently by the Receiving Party, even if unrelated to the Confidential Information, thus infringing on their pre-existing rights and creating a conflict with the limited rights granted in Section 16.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Survival. The restrictions and obligations of this Agreement shall continue and shall survive the termination of the Purpose of the disclosure for a period of five (5) years.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Survival. The restrictions and obligations of this Agreement shall continue and shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'of the Purpose of the disclosure for a period of five (5) years', it creates a potential for an in-text contradiction with Section 14, 'Entire Understanding'. If the 'Purpose of the disclosure' is no longer explicitly linked to the survival of the agreement, then there is no timeline defined, making the agreement now open ended. This contradicts the idea that the 'Entire Understanding' is fully contained in this document, implying there might be other understandings that define when the obligations end.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Entire Understanding. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding of the parties with respect to the specific subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes and replaces any and all prior understandings, arrangements and/or agreements, whether written or oral, relating to the Technology.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the defined timeline ('of the Purpose of the disclosure for a period of five (5) years') for the survival of the agreement, making it open-ended. This contradicts Section 14, 'Entire Understanding', because the agreement is no longer fully contained within the document, as there is no timeline. The omission creates a conflict because it introduces an external factor that might define the timeline, breaking the 'entire understanding' clause.
